because.text: Accepted in full. ADR-004: Codex was tasked to defend its Option 1 or concede; it conceded on the merits ('historical continuity cannot justify making a canonical result depend on blob state absent from node-CAS'), re-deriving the transitive-closure argument against its own prior position - with Kimi, Gemini and DeepSeek this closes the review gate at 4/>=3 for Option 2, zero dissent. No-blob-vectors rule adopted (blob vectors would reintroduce the external channel; strengthen EV-LIT-FORCE note + format note instead). ADR-005: R1 verdict with the missing technical substance - WavePin partial type (ph?/am?/en?), complete(interfere,pin) formulation, absent-wave semantics, five vectors; WV-FALSE-R1 {49152,0,-32512} and WV-FALSE-ANCESTOR-SILENT {49152,0,-32640} reproduced exactly against the oracle; wave-vector format extension need confirmed. Criterion answer counters Gemini's R2 use case: NodeHash/structural/phase addressing remains, zero mass is coherent, LORE non-normative.

subject.note: Codex ADR-004/005 gate review: Option 1 CONCEDED (004 gate closes at 4/>=3 Option 2); ADR-005 R1 + WavePin partial type
